text:
In the heart of a quiet forest under a velvet night sky, beneath an ancient oak whose leaves whispered secrets of centuries past, there lived an old gnome named Greedy. Greedy was unlike the other gnomes. He wasn't shy and not particularly modest either; he had a penchant for the absurd and a voracious appetite that rivaled that of a forest wolf. In a twist of fate and a misunderstanding of fairy tales, Greedy didn't particularly care for fairy tales.

One moonlit afternoon, as the stars danced in a waltz above the mossy ground, Greedy sat atop his favorite stump, peering at the twinkling dot of his house that had magically hovered above the entrance.

"Why would legends speak of honey when there's so much cream!" Greedy muttered, his cheeks puffing up so much his nose nearly popped through. That's when he saw it — a jar of cream, nestled among the thorns of a wild rose bush, clearly untouched and perfectly
